title: Protected Resource Authentication
statement: Implement a login API to authenticate users with JSON Web Tokens (JWT) and protect a resource endpoint. The login endpoint should accept username and password, verify the credentials, and return a JWT token if successful. The protected resource endpoint should require this JWT token to be passed in the Authorization header. Use the `jsonwebtoken` library to generate and verify JWT tokens. The login API should also support password reset and email verification features. The password reset feature should accept the new password and send a verification email with a link to reset the password. The email verification feature should send a verification link to the user's email address. The protected resource endpoint should return a 401 Unauthorized response if the JWT token is invalid or missing. Test the API with valid and invalid credentials, JWT tokens, and missing headers.
constraints: Must use `jsonwebtoken` library to generate and verify JWT tokens
Must use `express.json()` to parse request bodies
Password reset feature should send a verification email with a link to reset the password
Email verification feature should send a verification link to the user's email address
Protected resource endpoint should return 401 Unauthorized response if JWT token is invalid or missing

endpoints.description: Login user with username and password, returns JWT token on success
Reset user password with new password, sends verification email with link to reset password
Verify user email address, sends verification link to user's email address
Return protected resource if JWT token is valid and present in Authorization header
